VAL-SPEC-001.details: N-gram acceptance measured at 41.41% overall. By category: code 52.44%, json 17.77%, conversational 24.90%, repetitive 70.51%. Test methodology uses train/test split (correct). Acceptance rate below 50% target.
VAL-SPEC-001.methodology: Built n-gram cache from 60% of each prompt, measured acceptance on remaining 40%. Prevents training-on-test inflation.

VAL-SPEC-002.details: Test code bug: EAGLETestWrapper class missing 'engine' attribute. The eagle_speculative_decode function calls generator.engine.decode_step() but wrapper doesn't expose engine.
VAL-SPEC-002.methodology: Attempted to run actual GPU speculative decode but test wrapper implementation incomplete.

VAL-SPEC-003.details: Test code bug: TPInferenceEngine has prefill_step() not prefill(). Test code calls tp_engine.prefill() which doesn't exist.
VAL-SPEC-003.methodology: Attempted wall-clock timing comparison but test code incompatible with TP engine API.

frictions: Test wrapper classes (EAGLETestWrapper, SpeculativeTestWrapper) don't properly implement the interface expected by speculative decode functions
Test code assumes TPInferenceEngine.prefill() exists, but actual API is prefill_step()
notes: VAL-SPEC-001 ran successfully with correct methodology (train/test split)
N-gram acceptance varies significantly by prompt type (code: 52%, repetitive: 70%, json: 18%)
EAGLE infrastructure exists (EagleDraftHead, EagleSpeculativeGenerator classes) but test needs fixing
Speculative decode infrastructure is present but integration test code needs API fixes
